mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across consumer and capital demand channels, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio or fails to beat a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects immediate capacity utilization and manufacturing output, translating to positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, or returns are negative in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero policy rates and elevated unemployment forces central banks to maintain accommodative liquidity, which suppresses real discount rates and generates positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression or forward returns are negative in more than 60% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent indicates a broad-based demand contraction that compresses corporate profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 50% of sub-1.5% growth quarters over a 10-year historical sample.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly nonfarm payroll additions provide a direct leading indicator of household income growth and immediate consumer expenditure, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces a negative information coefficient or fails to predict direction in more than 48% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation while policy rates remain near zero raises expectations of premature monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict negative returns or shows a positive correlation with forward returns in out-of-sample validation.
